---
title: "standard HBMNL fit (single normal component, 2 chains) — saves full posterior"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
engine: jupyter
execute:
  timeout: 7200  # seconds; must exceed the slowest single fit (SLURM is the real wall-clock cap)
---

Fits the STANDARD (single-normal-component, no mixture) hierarchical Bayesian
multinomial logit model (Rossi section 5.4) to one simulated dataset (sampler /
chains / dataset come from the `params.csv` row `JOB_ROW`), then saves the **full
posterior** + `meta.json`. Every metric of the study's standard analysis notebook
(mu / POSTERIOR SIGMA incl. the empirical-covariance overlay / Delta / beta recovery,
per-parameter convergence, moments, marginal comparison on the full + Chebyshev
grids) is written per run as tidy CSVs via `src/summaries_standard.py`. Shared
model/sampler code is vendored from HierarchicalBayesianMultinomialLogit @ 893e63f
(`src/standardmodel.py`; default init, dense mass matrix on the Sigma block - the
standard runners have no informed-init option upstream).

CAPSLOCK variables are harness machinery — don't rename them.

## Parameters

```{python}
# | tags: [parameters]
JOB_DIR: str = "jobs/201-standard-hmc"
JOB_ROW: int = 0
JOB_TESTING: bool = True
```

## Setup

```{python}
# | label: imports
import io
import json
import logging
import pickle
import re
import sys
import time
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

print("python:", sys.executable)
print("cwd   :", Path.cwd())
```

```{python}
# | label: load-params-row
JOB_PATH = Path(JOB_DIR)
PARAMS = pd.read_csv(JOB_PATH / "params.csv").iloc[JOB_ROW, :]
print(PARAMS)
```

```{python}
# | label: identifier
now = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d-%H%M")
JOB_IDENTIFIER = JOB_PATH.name + "-" + f"{JOB_ROW:04d}" + "-" + now
PARAMS["job"] = JOB_PATH.name
PARAMS["run"] = JOB_IDENTIFIER
```

```{python}
# | label: directories
DIR_LOG = JOB_PATH / "log"
DIR_OUT = JOB_PATH / "out"
DIR_FIN = JOB_PATH / "finished"
if JOB_TESTING:
    DIR_OUT = JOB_PATH / "out-test"
for d in (DIR_LOG, DIR_OUT, DIR_FIN):
    d.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

FINFILE = DIR_FIN / str(JOB_ROW)
if FINFILE.exists() and not JOB_TESTING:
    raise RuntimeError(
        f"Row {JOB_ROW} of '{JOB_PATH.name}' is already finished. Delete {FINFILE} to re-run."
    )
```

```{python}
# | label: logger
formatter = logging.Formatter("%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s")
handler = logging.FileHandler(filename=DIR_LOG / f"run-{JOB_ROW:04d}.log")
handler.setFormatter(formatter)
logger = logging.getLogger(str(JOB_PATH.name))
logger.addHandler(handler)
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
logger.info(f"Job started: {JOB_IDENTIFIER}")
```

## Model code

### Make shared src/ importable

```{python}
# | label: import-vendored
JOB_PATH_ABS = JOB_PATH.resolve()
# Shared src/ is at the repo root locally (JOB_PATH/../..) and is symlinked into the
# node-local rundir on the HPC (JOB_PATH/src). Add both so `import src.*` resolves either way.
for _p in (JOB_PATH_ABS, JOB_PATH_ABS.parent.parent):
    if str(_p) not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, str(_p))

import jax  # noqa: F401
import jax.numpy as jnp

from src.standardmodel import (
    build_standard_hbmnl_model,
    run_nuts_inference_standard_hbmnl,
    run_hmc_inference_standard_hbmnl,
)

print("jax backend:", jax.default_backend())
```

### Extract run settings

```{python}
# | label: run-settings
dataset_key = str(PARAMS["dataset_key"])
sampler     = str(PARAMS["sampler"])
n_chains    = int(PARAMS["n_chains"])
warmup      = int(PARAMS["warmup"])
posterior   = int(PARAMS["posterior"])
seed        = int(PARAMS["seed"])
a_mu        = float(PARAMS["a_mu"])
a_delta     = float(PARAMS["a_delta"])
num_integration_steps = int(PARAMS["num_integration_steps"])

# Testing shortcut: short MCMC (still above goose's Stan-window minimum ~150).
if JOB_TESTING:
    warmup, posterior = 200, 100
    logger.info(f"JOB_TESTING: warmup={warmup}, posterior={posterior}")
```

### Load the dataset (with ground truth)

`data/in/` is two levels above the job dir — true locally (cwd = repo root) and on
the HPC (where `template.sh.j2` symlinks `data` next to the rundir).

```{python}
# | label: load-data
DATA_DIR = JOB_PATH_ABS.parent.parent / "data" / "in" / "standard_model"
data_path = DATA_DIR / f"{dataset_key}.json"
logger.info(f"Loading {data_path}")
with open(data_path) as f:
    raw = json.load(f)

P = int(raw["n_params"])
choice_data = {
    "X":        jnp.array(raw["X"]),
    "y":        jnp.array(raw["y"]),
    "unit_idx": jnp.array(raw["unit_idx"]),
    "Z":        jnp.array(raw["Z"]) if raw.get("Z") is not None else None,
    "n_params": P,
    "n_units":  int(raw["n_units"]),
    "n_demos":  int(raw.get("n_demos", 0)),
}
print(f"dataset={dataset_key}  N={choice_data['n_units']}  P={P}  D={choice_data['n_demos']}")
print(f"sampler={sampler}  chains={n_chains}  warmup={warmup}  posterior={posterior}")
```

### Build the model

```{python}
# | label: build-model
model = build_standard_hbmnl_model(
    data_dict=choice_data, A_delta=a_delta, a_mu=a_mu,
)
```

### Run the sampler (capturing the goose log)

```{python}
# | label: sample
log_buffer = io.StringIO()
_gh = logging.StreamHandler(log_buffer)
_gh.setFormatter(formatter)
_liesel_logger = logging.getLogger("liesel")
_liesel_logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
_liesel_logger.addHandler(_gh)

t0 = time.time()
if sampler == "nuts":
    mcmc_results, posterior_samples = run_nuts_inference_standard_hbmnl(
        model=model, chains=n_chains, warmup=warmup, posterior=posterior, seed=seed,
    )
elif sampler == "hmc":
    mcmc_results, posterior_samples = run_hmc_inference_standard_hbmnl(
        model=model, num_integration_steps=num_integration_steps,
        chains=n_chains, warmup=warmup, posterior=posterior, seed=seed,
    )
else:
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown sampler: {sampler}")
runtime_s = time.time() - t0

_liesel_logger.removeHandler(_gh)
sampling_log_text = log_buffer.getvalue()
logger.info(f"Sampling done in {runtime_s:.1f}s")
print(f"Sampling done in {runtime_s:.1f}s")
```

## Save the full posterior + meta

The complete posterior (chain structure `(C,S,…)` **+ `beta_i`**) uses the standard
model's plain keys (`mu`, `sigma_inv_chol_latent`, `Delta`, `beta_i` — no component
axis, no pvec). Tier-2 subset rule as in the mixture jobs.

```{python}
# | label: save-posterior-raw
RUN_KEY = f"{dataset_key}__standard_{sampler}_c{n_chains}"

# Tier-2 subset: keep the FULL posterior only for the first few replicate seeds (for
# cross-sampler comparisons needing raw chains together). EVERY run still writes the
# tidy per-run summaries below. See src/summaries_standard.py.
POSTERIOR_SUBSET_MAX = 5
data_seed = int(PARAMS.get("data_seed", -1))
SAVE_POSTERIOR = JOB_TESTING or data_seed <= POSTERIOR_SUBSET_MAX

post_np = {k: np.asarray(v) for k, v in posterior_samples.items()}
if SAVE_POSTERIOR:
    DIR_POST = DIR_OUT / "posterior_raw"
    DIR_POST.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with open(DIR_POST / f"{RUN_KEY}.pkl", "wb") as f:
        pickle.dump(post_np, f)
    print("saved posterior_raw:", {k: tuple(v.shape) for k, v in post_np.items()})
else:
    print(f"posterior_raw NOT saved (data_seed={data_seed} > {POSTERIOR_SUBSET_MAX}); on-node summaries only")
```

## Save sampler diagnostics

Per-kernel transition diagnostics from Goose (NUTS: `divergent`/`treedepth`/`leapfrog`/
`acceptance_prob`; HMC: `divergent`/`acceptance_prob`/`position_moved`), arrays
`(chains, draws)`. Pure post-hoc extraction from `mcmc_results`.

```{python}
# | label: save-diagnostics
import dataclasses

def _extract_diagnostics(results):
    try:
        tis = results.get_posterior_transition_infos()   # {kernel_id: TransitionInfo}
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning(f"transition infos unavailable: {e}")
        return {}, {}
    out = {}
    for kid, info in tis.items():
        out[kid] = {f.name: np.asarray(getattr(info, f.name))
                    for f in dataclasses.fields(info)}
    try:
        kbp = dict(results.get_kernels_by_pos_key())     # {pos_key: kernel_id}
    except Exception:
        kbp = {}
    return out, kbp

diag, kernels_by_pos_key = _extract_diagnostics(mcmc_results)
DIR_DIAG = DIR_OUT / "diagnostics"
DIR_DIAG.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
with open(DIR_DIAG / f"{RUN_KEY}.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(
        {"sampler": sampler, "run_key": RUN_KEY,
         "kernels_by_pos_key": kernels_by_pos_key,  # {pos_key: kernel_id}
         "transition_infos": diag}, f)              # {kernel_id: {field: (chains, draws)}}

def _diag_rollup(diag):
    n_div, max_td, max_lp, accs = 0, None, None, []
    for fl in diag.values():
        if "divergent" in fl:
            n_div += int(np.sum(fl["divergent"]))
        if "treedepth" in fl:
            t = int(np.max(fl["treedepth"])); max_td = t if max_td is None else max(max_td, t)
        if "leapfrog" in fl:
            l = int(np.max(fl["leapfrog"])); max_lp = l if max_lp is None else max(max_lp, l)
        if "acceptance_prob" in fl:
            accs.append(float(np.mean(fl["acceptance_prob"])))
    mean_acc = round(float(np.mean(accs)), 4) if accs else None
    return n_div, max_td, max_lp, mean_acc

n_divergent, max_treedepth, max_leapfrog, mean_acceptance = _diag_rollup(diag)
print("diagnostics:", {"n_divergent": n_divergent, "max_treedepth": max_treedepth,
                       "max_leapfrog": max_leapfrog, "mean_acceptance": mean_acceptance})
```

```{python}
# | label: save-meta
def _count_sampling_errors(log_text):
    total = 0
    for line in log_text.splitlines():
        if "Errors per chain" in line:
            m = re.search(r":\s*(\d+)\s*/\s*\d+\s*transitions", line)
            if m:
                total += int(m.group(1))
    return total

meta = {
    "job": str(PARAMS["job"]), "run": str(PARAMS["run"]), "job_row": int(JOB_ROW),
    "run_key": RUN_KEY, "model": "standard_hbmnl",
    "dataset_key": dataset_key, "scenario": str(PARAMS.get("scenario", "standard")),
    "k_true": 1, "data_seed": int(PARAMS.get("data_seed", -1)),
    "k_model": 1, "sampler": sampler, "n_chains": n_chains,
    "warmup": warmup, "posterior": posterior, "seed": seed,
    "a_mu": a_mu, "a_delta": a_delta,
    "num_integration_steps": num_integration_steps if sampler == "hmc" else None,
    "n_units": choice_data["n_units"], "n_params": P, "n_demos": choice_data["n_demos"],
    "runtime_s": round(runtime_s, 2),
    "n_sampling_errors": _count_sampling_errors(sampling_log_text),
    "n_divergent": n_divergent, "max_treedepth": max_treedepth,
    "max_leapfrog": max_leapfrog, "mean_acceptance": mean_acceptance,
    "posterior_shapes": {k: list(v.shape) for k, v in post_np.items()},
    "finished_at": datetime.now().isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
}
DIR_META = DIR_OUT / "meta"
DIR_META.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
with open(DIR_META / f"{RUN_KEY}.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(meta, f, indent=2)
print("meta:", json.dumps({k: meta[k] for k in
      ("run_key", "runtime_s", "n_sampling_errors", "n_divergent", "mean_acceptance")}))
```

## Save per-run summary tables (Tier 1)

Every tidy table computable from THIS run's posterior + ground truth is written as
`out/<table>/<run_key>.csv`: mu / SIGMA (incl. empirical covariance) / Delta / beta
recovery, per-parameter convergence (R-hat/ESS for mu, every Sigma element, tr(Sigma),
Delta and the notebook's two inspected households), moments, marginal distances and
density diagnostics on BOTH grid scenarios, and sampler diagnostics. The same
`src/summaries_standard.py` builds them on-node here AND post-hoc, so per-run CSVs
and gathered tables agree exactly. If summarising fails, the posterior is saved as a
fallback so an expensive run is never lost.

```{python}
# | label: save-summaries
from src import summaries_standard as smry

diag_payload = {"kernels_by_pos_key": kernels_by_pos_key, "transition_infos": diag}
try:
    summary_tables, _ = smry.per_run_tables(post_np, meta, raw, diag_payload)
    for _name, _rows in summary_tables.items():
        if not _rows:
            continue
        _dir = DIR_OUT / _name
        _dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        pd.DataFrame(_rows).to_csv(_dir / f"{RUN_KEY}.csv", index=False)
    logger.info("per-run summaries: " + ", ".join(f"{k}={len(v)}" for k, v in summary_tables.items()))
    print("saved summaries:", {k: len(v) for k, v in summary_tables.items()})
except Exception as e:  # never lose a run to a summary bug: keep the posterior to re-derive
    logger.error(f"on-node summaries failed: {e}")
    if not SAVE_POSTERIOR:
        DIR_POST = DIR_OUT / "posterior_raw"
        DIR_POST.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with open(DIR_POST / f"{RUN_KEY}.pkl", "wb") as f:
            pickle.dump(post_np, f)
        logger.error("saved posterior_raw as fallback for post-hoc re-derivation")
    print(f"WARNING: on-node summaries failed ({e}); see log")
```

## Mark job as finished

```{python}
# | label: mark-finished
if not JOB_TESTING:
    FINFILE.touch()
    logger.info("Marked finished.")
```
